task: Create the folder src/middleware if it does not exist, then create src/middleware/fortress.js that exports: (1) an Express middleware function setting the security headers (and HSTS if FORTRESS_HSTS=true), (2) a Koa middleware (async (ctx, next)) setting the same headers, and (3) an Express Router exported as router with GET /status/fo returning 200 JSON { status: "ok", fortress: true }, using CommonJS. Do not modify other files yet.

subtasks.description: Create src/middleware/fortress.js implementing CommonJS exports: (a) Express middleware that sets security headers and conditionally Strict-Transport-Security when process.env.FORTRESS_HSTS==='true'; (b) Koa middleware async (ctx, next) that sets the same headers and conditional HSTS; (c) an Express Router with GET /status/fo returning JSON { status: 'ok', fortress: true }. Use CommonJS exports: module.exports = { middleware, koaMiddleware, router }.
subtasks.acceptance_criteria: File created at src/middleware/fortress.js using CommonJS. Requiring it (node -e) does not throw. Exports an object with keys 'middleware' (function), 'koaMiddleware' (async function), and 'router' (an Express Router). The router defines GET /status/fo returning JSON { status: 'ok', fortress: true }.

changes.explanation: Create new CommonJS module exporting Express middleware, Koa middleware, and an Express Router with /status/fo endpoint. Sets security headers with conditional HSTS based on process.env.FORTRESS_HSTS.

subtasks.description: Edit src/server.js to require the fortress module and apply the Express middleware early, before other routes, and mount the fortress router at root. Also ensure the Express app is exported (module.exports = app) if not already exported so tests can import it. Keep edits minimal and preserve existing functionality.
subtasks.acceptance_criteria: src/server.js is modified to require('./src/middleware/fortress.js') (or equivalent) and to call app.use(fortress.middleware) (or app.use(fortress)) as early as possible after app is created and before other routes. The router is mounted with app.use('/', fortress.router). Requiring src/server.js does not throw. If previously not exporting the app, the file now exports the Express app via module.exports = app (preserving existing behavior if it already exported).

subtasks.description: Add Jest + SuperTest test file test/test_fortress.test.js that requires the Express app and verifies GET /status/fo returns 200 and JSON body { status: 'ok', fortress: true }, asserts response contains headers X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, X-XSS-Protection, Referrer-Policy, and when process.env.FORTRESS_HSTS='true' is set (for that test case) Strict-Transport-Security header is present.
subtasks.acceptance_criteria: test/test_fortress.test.js exists and running 'npx jest --runTestsByPath test/test_fortress.test.js' executes the test file. The tests include two scenarios: default (no FORTRESS_HSTS) asserting required security headers exist, and with process.env.FORTRESS_HSTS='true' asserting Strict-Transport-Security header exists. Tests pass against the app exported from src/server.js (or by requiring server.js).

plan_summary: Add a CommonJS 'fortress' middleware module providing Express and Koa middleware plus an Express Router, wire it into the existing Express server early, and add a Jest+SuperTest test to verify route and headers (including optional HSTS via environment variable).
risk_assessment: Medium -- changes to src/server.js must be minimal but may differ across projects; ensuring module.exports = app may modify existing behavior. Tests depend on the server exporting the app for SuperTest; if the existing server is structured differently additional minor adjustments might be needed.
